calendar.matters:
MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar Mar-07-2022 Vacated

Progress Report On The Deposit Of Money Into A Blocked Account For Mira Shelub
Off Calendar Feb-04-2022 Off Calendar

Hearing On The Petition To Approve The Compromise Of The Claim Or Action Of A Person With A Disability
Off Calendar Nov-22-2021 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: This matter came on calendar this day for hearing on the Petition to Approve Compromise on the Claim or Action of a Person with a Disability.
Dept. 514. Judge: Murlene J. Randle. Clerk: Kevin Dougherty. Proceedings recorded by "For the Record" commencing at 10:17 a.m.

Annie Wu, Esq. appeared with petitioner Elaine Shelub, who was present as Guardian ad Litem for Mira Shelub, who was not present.
Petitioner was placed under oath and testified.
Bot Ms. Wu and Ms. Shelub appeared via ZOOM for this morning's hearing.
Order appointing Elaine Shelub Guardian ad Litem for Mira Shelub was filed August 10, 2021.

On the basis of evidence received, and good cause appearing, the Petitions are granted and the Compromises are approved as follows:

Settlement Amount - $700,000.00.
Attorney Fees - $166,268.98.
Medical Bills/Liens - $74,130.20.
Costs - $34,924.08.

The balance of $424,676.74* shall be deposited into a Blocked Account with Bank of America, Stonestown Branch, located at 245 Winston Drive, San Francisco, CA 94132. No withdrawals shall be made without a written Court Order.

Counsel for petitioner shall prepare the proposed Orders Approving the Compromise and to Deposit Money into the Blocked Account and submit same to the Court for review and signature.

Matter is CONTINUED to February 8, 2022 at 9:00 a.m. in Department 514 for a Progress Report on the Deposit of Money into the Blocked Accounts.

Proceedings concluded at 10:55 a.m.

*With the Court's approval, petitioner shall make a determination as to how much of the $424,676.74 is required to address Mira Shelub's present economic needs, and upon approval the Court shall deduct said amount from the balance prior to depositing the remainer into the Blocked Account.
